post.description:
Create a new URL alias for the current customer.

If ``is_routing`` is True (the default) and another alias is already
routing the same (name, request_routing_key), this one is created as
non-routing instead. Users can later switch routing via the
``/aliases/{id}/switch`` endpoint.

Combined routing-object cap (#1071 / #1083 / #1136): when the
customer's active plan declares ``terms.routing_objects.max_allowed``,
creating past that cap — counting ALL the customer's routing objects
(aliases + collections) — returns 422 with an upgrade-prompt body.
When ``max_allowed`` is ``None`` (unlimited), creation is always
allowed. Routing objects are free; there is no per-object billing.
Seat-scaling for Team subscriptions is applied by
``enforce_quota_for_customer``. Unsubscribed customers have no quota.

parameters.name: shared

schema.description: When true, create a shared team alias visible/editable by all org members. When false (default), create a personal alias owned by this user.
schema.title: Shared

parameters.description: When true, create a shared team alias visible/editable by all org members. When false (default), create a personal alias owned by this user.

get.description: List customer's aliases, optionally filtered by name.

parameters.name: shared

schema.description: When true, list shared team aliases only. When false (default), list the caller's personal aliases plus shared ones.
schema.title: Shared

parameters.description: When true, list shared team aliases only. When false (default), list the caller's personal aliases plus shared ones.

get.description: Get a specific alias by ID.

patch.description: Update an alias's target path or routing key override.

delete.description: Deactivate an alias (sets deactivated_at for billing proration).

post.description:
Switch routing on or off for this alias.

When ``on=True``, any sibling alias currently routing the same
(name, routing_key) combo is atomically demoted. The response
includes ``demoted_alias_id`` so the caller knows what changed.

When ``on=False``, the alias stops routing. No sibling is
auto-promoted — the combo will have no routing alias until one
is explicitly switched on.

Billing is unaffected — all non-deactivated aliases accrue duration
regardless of routing state.
Create a new broadcast fan-out config.

parameters.name: shared

schema.description: Create a shared team broadcast (owner_id=NULL) instead of a personal broadcast owned by the caller.
schema.title: Shared

parameters.description: Create a shared team broadcast (owner_id=NULL) instead of a personal broadcast owned by the caller.

get.description: List customer's broadcasts.

parameters.name: shared

schema.description: Return only shared team broadcasts (owner_id=NULL). Default returns the caller's personal broadcasts plus shared ones.
schema.title: Shared

parameters.description: Return only shared team broadcasts (owner_id=NULL). Default returns the caller's personal broadcasts plus shared ones.

get.description: Get a specific broadcast by ID.

patch.description: Update a broadcast's config (mode, timeout, enabled, description).

delete.description: Delete a broadcast and all its targets.

put.description: Replace the full target set for a broadcast.

post.description: Add one target to a broadcast.

delete.description: Remove one target from a broadcast.

post.description: Create a new sequential dispatch chain.

parameters.name: shared

schema.description: Create a shared team chain (owner_id=NULL) instead of a personal chain owned by the caller.
schema.title: Shared

parameters.description: Create a shared team chain (owner_id=NULL) instead of a personal chain owned by the caller.

get.description: List customer's chains.

parameters.name: shared

schema.description: Return only shared team chains (owner_id=NULL). Default returns the caller's personal chains plus shared ones.
schema.title: Shared

parameters.description: Return only shared team chains (owner_id=NULL). Default returns the caller's personal chains plus shared ones.

get.description: Get a specific chain by ID.

patch.description: Update a chain's config (default_timeout_ms, enabled, description).

delete.description: Delete a chain and all its steps.

put.description: Replace the full step list for a chain.

post.description: Append one step to a chain.

patch.description: Update one step's fields (target, conditions, timeout, sort_order).

delete.description: Remove one step from a chain.

post.description:
Enroll in a service asynchronously.

Creates a pending enrollment and dispatches a Celery task to activate it.
Returns a task_id for polling via GET /tasks/{task_id}.

Requires X-Role-Id header (JWT) or a svcpass customer API key with
customer context.

parameters.name: shared

schema.description: When true, create a shared team enrollment visible to all org members. The default creates a personal enrollment for the caller.
schema.title: Shared

parameters.description: When true, create a shared team enrollment visible to all org members. The default creates a personal enrollment for the caller.

get.description:
List user's service enrollments with optional enriched service data.

Uses ServiceMView for efficient data retrieval when include_service_details=True.

parameters.name: shared

schema.description: When true (default), list shared team enrollments. When false, list the caller's personal enrollments. The default preserves legacy shared enrollment visibility during the migration.
schema.title: Shared

parameters.description: When true (default), list shared team enrollments. When false, list the caller's personal enrollments. The default preserves legacy shared enrollment visibility during the migration.

get.description:
Get a specific enrollment by ID with enriched service data.
Users can only access their own customer's enrollments.

Uses ServiceMView and AccessInterface resolution for comprehensive data.

delete.description:
Cancel (unenroll from) an enrollment.

Sets status to ``cancelled`` and invalidates the gateway customer
context cache so in-flight requests stop being proxied. The enrollment
row and its AccessInterfaces are preserved for potential
reactivation via re-enrollment with matching parameters.

Callers can only cancel their own customer's enrollments.

get.description:
List service groups visible to the customer as a ``{data, count}``
envelope.

Merges two kinds of rows into one ``CustomerGroupView`` shape under
``data`` (``count`` is ``len(data)``):

- **Platform groups** (``owner_type="platform"``, ``editable=False``)
  — active platform groups with at least one service. Excludes
  draft/archived/private groups, empty nodes, and seller-owned
  groups. ``member_count`` comes from the cached ``num_services``.
  Ordered with ``misc`` groups last, then by ``sort_order``, then
  alphabetically by name — matching the marketplace browse order.
- **Own collections** (``owner_type="customer"``, ``editable=True``)
  — the customer's own ``ServiceCollection`` rows. ``member_count``
  is counted live.

The ``owner`` filter narrows this: ``system`` returns platform
groups only, ``own`` returns collections only, ``all`` (default)
returns both (platform first, then collections).

parameters.name: name

schema.description: Filter by name (partial match)
schema.title: Name

parameters.description: Filter by name (partial match)

parameters.name: owner

schema.description: Which rows to return: 'all' (platform + own), 'system' (platform only), or 'own' (collections only).
schema.title: Owner

parameters.description: Which rows to return: 'all' (platform + own), 'system' (platform only), or 'own' (collections only).

parameters.name: shared

schema.description: When true, list shared team collections only. When false (default), list the caller's personal collections plus shared ones.
schema.title: Shared

parameters.description: When true, list shared team collections only. When false (default), list the caller's personal collections plus shared ones.

post.description:
Create a customer-owned service collection.

Collections are the editable half of the unified ``/customer/groups``
surface: customer-curated catalogs addressable at ``/g/<name>``.
Platform groups remain read-only here. The ``name`` slug is unique
per owner scope; a duplicate in the same scope returns 409. A personal
collection may coexist with a same-named shared one (#1471).

parameters.name: shared

schema.description: When true, create a shared team collection visible/editable by all org members. When false (default), create a personal collection owned by this user.
schema.title: Shared

parameters.description: When true, create a shared team collection visible/editable by all org members. When false (default), create a personal collection owned by this user.

patch.description:
Update a customer-owned collection.

Only the customer's own collections are editable. Platform groups
are addressable by id or name but read-only: a UUID mapping to a
visible platform group returns 403 ("Platform groups are
read-only"); a UUID mapping to nothing visible returns 404.

delete.description:
Delete a customer-owned collection.

Only the customer's own collections can be deleted. Platform groups
are addressable by id or name but read-only: a UUID mapping to a
visible platform group returns 403 ("Platform groups are
read-only"); a UUID mapping to nothing visible returns 404.

get.description:
Get a single group from the unified surface, by name or UUID.

Both kinds resolve to one unified ``CustomerGroupDetail`` shape:

1. If ``name`` parses as a UUID that maps to one of the customer's
   own collections, return the collection
   (``owner_type="customer"``, ``editable=True``, ``enabled`` set,
   ``group_type="collection"``; interface / routing_policy null).
2. Otherwise resolve a visible platform group by ``id`` or ``name``
   and return it (``owner_type="platform"``, ``editable=False``,
   with embedded interface / routing policy; ``enabled`` null).
   Both ``id`` and ``name`` are returned in the shape, so platform
   groups are addressable by either; ``name`` is the stable handle
   that survives admin recreations.

Collections are UUID-keyed (their slug is reserved for the future
``/g/<name>`` dispatch path). Returns 404 for anything outside the
customer-visible set — existence is not leaked.

List customer-visible services that belong to a group.

The group is addressable by id or name; both are returned in the
group detail shape. Names stay valid across admin recreations of a
group, so SDK scripts that use the name keep working.
Mirrors the visibility rule used by the GraphQL
``resolve_group_services`` resolver for non-admin / non-seller
callers: service must be ``status='active'`` and
``visibility='public'``. The group itself must be in the
customer-visible set (active + platform + non-empty).

Uses keyset pagination on ``(created_at DESC, service_id DESC)``
to match the seller ``services_list`` endpoint — clients echo
``next_cursor`` back unchanged to fetch subsequent pages.

parameters.name: cursor

schema.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a previous response's `next_cursor`. Omit to start from the first page.
schema.title: Cursor

parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a previous response's `next_cursor`. Omit to start from the first page.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Page size (default 50, max 200).
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Page size (default 50, max 200).

parameters.name: search

schema.description: Case-insensitive substring match on name, display_name, or provider_name.
schema.title: Search

parameters.description: Case-insensitive substring match on name, display_name, or provider_name.

get.description: List the member services of a customer-owned collection.

post.description:
Add a service to a customer-owned collection.

The service must be one the customer can dispatch — it needs no
enrollment, or the customer has an active enrollment for it.

delete.description: Remove a service from a customer-owned collection.

post.description:
Create a draft recurrent request.

Returns the created record with its ID, which can be used as the
X-Playground-Request header for test requests.

get.description: List recurrent requests for the current customer.
Get a recurrent request by ID.

patch.description:
Update a recurrent request (template, schedule, name, or status).

Setting a schedule on a draft transitions it to active and creates
a RedBeat entry for periodic execution.

delete.description: Delete a recurrent request and remove its RedBeat entry.

post.description:
Execute a recurrent request once via the gateway proxy.

Resolves body templates, sends the request through APISIX (same path a
live request takes, signed with the customer's platform key), and returns
the upstream response directly (#646).

get.description: List request logs for the authenticated customer. Returns lightweight columns (no body/header fields) for fast pagination.

schema.description: Filter to firings produced by a specific RecurrentRequest (scheduled execution). When set, returns only rows whose ClickHouse ``recurrent_request_id`` column matches this UUID — drives the 'View executions' drill-down on the Scheduled Requests page. Pre-existing rows from before #1141 have an empty ``recurrent_request_id`` and are naturally excluded.

parameters.description: Filter to firings produced by a specific RecurrentRequest (scheduled execution). When set, returns only rows whose ClickHouse ``recurrent_request_id`` column matches this UUID — drives the 'View executions' drill-down on the Scheduled Requests page. Pre-existing rows from before #1141 have an empty ``recurrent_request_id`` and are naturally excluded.

post.description: Enable request logging for the authenticated user.

parameters.name: truncate_long_message

schema.description:
Picks the mode. ``True`` → ``truncated`` (8 KB inline preview, no S3). ``False`` → ``complete`` (full body uploaded to S3 for retrieval via the detail endpoint). ``None`` (default) → preserve the user's existing ``preference.logging.enabled`` if it's already ``truncated`` or ``complete``; fall back to ``truncated`` otherwise.

Two intended caller shapes:

- **Frontend**: sets ``preference.logging`` via   ``PATCH /users/me`` (composing with ``expire_at`` etc.),   then calls /start with no param to flip the gateway on   using the just-stored mode.
- **SDK / scripts**: pass ``True`` or ``False``   explicitly — the SDK has no concept of the customer's   stored preference, so it picks the mode per call.
schema.title: Truncate Long Message

parameters.description:
Picks the mode. ``True`` → ``truncated`` (8 KB inline preview, no S3). ``False`` → ``complete`` (full body uploaded to S3 for retrieval via the detail endpoint). ``None`` (default) → preserve the user's existing ``preference.logging.enabled`` if it's already ``truncated`` or ``complete``; fall back to ``truncated`` otherwise.

Two intended caller shapes:

- **Frontend**: sets ``preference.logging`` via   ``PATCH /users/me`` (composing with ``expire_at`` etc.),   then calls /start with no param to flip the gateway on   using the just-stored mode.
- **SDK / scripts**: pass ``True`` or ``False``   explicitly — the SDK has no concept of the customer's   stored preference, so it picks the mode per call.

post.description: Disable request logging for the authenticated user.

get.description: Get full detail of a single request log, including request/response bodies.

post.description:
Dry-run resolve a gateway path + routing key to its candidate services.

Mirrors the gateway's decision without executing or caching the
result. Response shape is customer-safe — no wallet, no secrets,
no upstream credentials.

get.description:
List the current customer's secrets (shared + personal).

Returns secret metadata only — values are never returned. ``limit``
defaults to ``None`` (every secret); pass one to paginate.

Get metadata for one of the current customer's secrets by name.

Returns the effective secret for the caller — personal takes precedence
over shared. Returns metadata only — the value is never returned.

put.description:
Set a customer secret to ``value`` (idempotent create-or-replace).

Returns ``201 Created`` on insert and ``200 OK`` on update.
The value is encrypted at rest. **The value cannot be retrieved
after this call** — store it securely if you need a copy.

The customer's context cache is invalidated so the gateway picks
up the new value immediately.

parameters.name: shared

schema.description: When true, set the shared team secret visible to all org members. When false (default), set a personal secret visible only to this user. Personal secrets shadow same-named shared secrets for your requests.
schema.title: Shared

parameters.description: When true, set the shared team secret visible to all org members. When false (default), set a personal secret visible only to this user. Personal secrets shadow same-named shared secrets for your requests.

delete.description:
Delete a customer secret by name.

**Warning:** this action cannot be undone. Any active enrollments
that reference this secret will stop working immediately. The
customer's context cache is invalidated.

parameters.name: shared

schema.description: When true, delete the shared team secret. When false (default), delete the caller's personal secret.
schema.title: Shared

parameters.description: When true, delete the shared team secret. When false (default), delete the caller's personal secret.

get.description:
Get details of a single service visible to the customer.

Hot path: read from ``service_mview`` for active + public services.

Cold path: if the mview misses, fall back to a base-tables query
that matches ``status = 'deprecated' AND visibility = 'public'``.
This enables statement / invoice back-reference URLs to keep
rendering after a service has been deprecated (the service still
exists in the base tables; only the mview filters it out). See
unitysvc#1027.

Returns 404 for non-public services, or for statuses other than
``active`` / ``deprecated`` — the visible set is intentionally
narrow so the endpoint doesn't leak the existence of unlisted
or draft services.

List access interfaces dispatchable by the calling customer.

Returns two kinds of interfaces, unified so the SDK can present
them as a single ``service.interfaces()`` list:

1. **Shared** interfaces (``enrollment_id IS NULL``) — usable by
   any customer, matches the public playground behaviour.
2. **Enrollment-bound** interfaces whose ``enrollment_id`` belongs
   to an enrollment owned by the authenticated customer. BYOK /
   BYOE services create these on enrollment activation so the
   gateway can substitute customer-supplied secrets at runtime.

Interfaces bound to *other* customers' enrollments are filtered
out — existence is not leaked. ``base_url`` is resolved with
``settings.resolve_gateway_urls`` so SDK consumers can dispatch
directly without knowing gateway substitution rules.

Return the customer's editable collections flagged with whether
they contain this service, plus eligibility and a synthetic
Favorites entry.

put.description:
Set which of the customer's editable collections contain the
service (toggle/diff). ``include_default`` ensures the Favorites
collection exists then adds; unchecking removes. Rejects services the
customer can't dispatch.

AccessInterface.title: AccessInterface
AccessInterface.description:
Access interface entry exposed to customers.

Identified by ``name`` (per ``service`` for service-bound
interfaces, per ``group`` for group-level ones). The DB primary
key is intentionally not exposed — interface UUIDs change when
admins recreate parent rows, so an ``id`` field would tempt SDK
consumers to cache an unstable handle.

The actual ``api_key`` is never included — secrets are
write-only. ``base_url`` is resolved against the active gateway
config so the client can dispatch without further substitution.

Scoping fields let the SDK filter the returned list:

- ``enrollment_id`` — set when the interface is bound to a
  specific enrollment (BYOK/BYOE services); ``None`` for shared
  interfaces. SDK uses this for
  ``service.dispatch(enrollment=enr)`` resolution.
- ``group_name`` — set when the interface is declared on a
  service group rather than on the service itself; ``None`` for
  per-service interfaces. Identified by name (not UUID) because
  group UUIDs are not stable across admin recreations.

Intentionally omits:
- ``is_primary`` — multiple public interfaces on the same
  service map to the same upstream, so ``service.dispatch()``
  auto-picks one and a "primary" flag is unnecessary. The
  ``interface=`` argument on ``service.dispatch()`` is only
  needed to disambiguate when the customer has multiple
  enrollments on the service.
- ``is_active`` — only active interfaces are returned, so the
  field would always be ``True``.
- ``sort_order`` — seller-internal ordering hint, used to
  sort this response but not surfaced to the customer.

CustomerEnrollment.title: CustomerEnrollment
CustomerEnrollment.description:
Customer-visible enrollment record.

``parameters`` is always sanitized — keys matching common secret
names (``api_key``, ``password``, ...) are returned as
``***masked***`` so the SDK never round-trips raw secrets.
``service`` and ``proxy_endpoint`` are present when the list/get
endpoint is called with ``include_service_details=True`` (the
default).

CustomerEnrollmentCancelResponse.title: CustomerEnrollmentCancelResponse
CustomerEnrollmentCancelResponse.description: Response after cancelling (unenrolling from) an enrollment.

CustomerEnrollmentCreateResponse.title: CustomerEnrollmentCreateResponse
CustomerEnrollmentCreateResponse.description:
Async enrollment ack. Poll the ``task_id`` via ``/tasks/{id}``
to observe activation progress, or fetch the enrollment later
to check its ``status``.

CustomerEnrollmentsResponse.title: CustomerEnrollmentsResponse
CustomerEnrollmentsResponse.description: Paginated list of the caller's enrollments.

CustomerGroupDetail.title: CustomerGroupDetail
CustomerGroupDetail.description:
Unified detail shape for a single group from /customer/groups — a
read-only platform group or the customer's own editable collection.
Same fields for both; type-specific fields are null for the other type.

CustomerGroupListResponse.title: CustomerGroupListResponse
CustomerGroupListResponse.description:
Envelope for the unified groups list. ``data`` items are
``CustomerGroupView`` (platform + own); ``count`` is len(data).

CustomerGroupMembershipEntry.title: CustomerGroupMembershipEntry
CustomerGroupMembershipEntry.description:
One row in the Add-to-Group dropdown: a customer-editable
collection (or the synthetic Favorites entry) flagged with whether
the target service is currently a member.

CustomerGroupMembershipUpdate.title: CustomerGroupMembershipUpdate
CustomerGroupMembershipUpdate.description:
PUT body: the collections that should contain the service after
save. ``group_ids`` are the customer's editable collections;
``include_default`` toggles the Favorites collection (created on
first use).

CustomerGroupView.title: CustomerGroupView
CustomerGroupView.description:
Unified shape for the merged /customer/groups list — either a read-only
platform ServiceGroup or the customer's own editable ServiceCollection.

GatewayKind.title: GatewayKind
GatewayKind.description:
Short-form gateway selector for ``ResolveRequest.gateway``.

Customer-facing shorthand — maps to the ``<NAME>_GATEWAY_BASE_URL``
settings template internally. ``api`` covers HTTP services (LLM,
uptime monitors, generic APIs); ``s3`` and ``smtp`` cover storage
and email relay.

GroupTypeEnum.title: GroupTypeEnum
GroupTypeEnum.description:
Type of service group — derived from configuration, not set directly.

Derivation rules:
- No rules, no access interfaces → category (organizes descendants)
- Rules, no access interfaces → collection (curated set for browsing)
- Rules + access interfaces → group (has own API endpoint + routing)
- System-generated catch-all → misc
- Set explicitly (never derived, like misc) → capability_pool

``capability_pool`` (#1244) backs the ``/p/<name>`` namespace: membership
is claim-driven — services instantiated from a ServiceTemplate whose
``pool_name`` matches the group name — computed by a dedicated refresh,
not by ``membership_rules``. Distinct from ``routable`` (``/g/``), whose
membership is curated.

OpsCustomerRequestLogDetail.title: OpsCustomerRequestLogDetail
OpsCustomerRequestLogDetail.description:
Customer request-log detail for ops-customer members.

Sellers who are active members of the configured ops customer team may
inspect the gateway-transformed upstream request for their own routing
diagnostics. Regular customers still receive ``RequestLogDetail`` without
this field.

RecurrentRequestCreate.title: RecurrentRequestCreate
RecurrentRequestCreate.description:
Schema for creating a RecurrentRequest (draft).

``request_headers`` is the raw dict of headers sent on each execution
and is the **single source of truth** for per-request opt-ins such as
``X-Unitysvc-Log-Request``. Higher-level clients (frontend, CLI) are
responsible for computing the final dict — this schema does not
accept any convenience flags that would mutate it server-side.

RecurrentRequestStatusEnum.title: RecurrentRequestStatusEnum
RecurrentRequestStatusEnum.description:
Status of a recurrent request.

Lifecycle: draft → active → paused → cancelled

RecurrentRequestUpdate.title: RecurrentRequestUpdate
RecurrentRequestUpdate.description:
Schema for updating a RecurrentRequest.

``request_headers`` replaces the stored dict wholesale when provided
(partial/PATCH semantics apply at the top level, not inside the dict).
See :class:`RecurrentRequestCreate` for the rationale.

RecurrentRequestsPublic.title: RecurrentRequestsPublic
RecurrentRequestsPublic.description: List of RecurrentRequests for API responses.

RequestLogDetail.title: RequestLogDetail
RequestLogDetail.description:
Customer-facing request log detail (#1032 / #882).

Includes the customer's own request and the upstream response —
the two pieces a customer needs to debug their own traffic.
Deliberately omits ``gateway_request`` (transformed body, upstream
URL, seller's upstream credentials) since exposing it would leak
the seller's upstream identity and credential material to customers.

Bodies are returned truncated to the inline cap by default. When
``truncate_long_message=false`` and the body was stored in full
(``complete`` mode at write-time), the detail endpoint fetches the
full body from S3 and substitutes it into the response — the
shape stays the same.

RequestLogListItem.title: RequestLogListItem
RequestLogListItem.description: Lightweight request log item for list/pagination views.

RequestLogListResponse.title: RequestLogListResponse
RequestLogListResponse.description: Paginated list of request log items.

ResolveCandidate.title: ResolveCandidate
ResolveCandidate.description:
One service the gateway would route to, customer-safe subset.

Omits operational fields (upstream_config with resolved API keys,
transformers, response_rules, seller_id, pricing_bundle_id) — those
are gateway internals. The SDK gets identity + the enrollment
binding it needs to reason about dispatch.

path.title: Path
path.description: Gateway request path without the gateway base URL — same shape as what ``service.dispatch(path=...)`` / ``group.dispatch(path=...)`` produces. Examples: ``v1/chat/completions``, ``openai``, ``a/my-alias/foo``.

routing_key.title: Routing Key
routing_key.description: Optional routing key the gateway would match against interface ``routing_key`` rules (e.g. ``{'model': 'gpt-4'}``). Omit to see every interface matching the path.

strategy.title: Strategy
strategy.description: Override the group's configured routing strategy (e.g. ``by_price``, ``lowest_latency``). Omit to use the strategy declared on the group's ``routing_policy``. ``weighted_random`` is the implicit baseline.

gateway.description: Which gateway the path belongs to — ``api`` (default, HTTP services like LLM / uptime), ``s3`` (storage), or ``smtp`` (email relay). Internally mapped to the corresponding ``<NAME>_GATEWAY_BASE_URL`` setting so customers don't have to know the env-var naming.

ResolveRequest.title: ResolveRequest
ResolveRequest.description: Dry-run resolution input, mirroring what the gateway accepts.

routing_strategy.title: Routing Strategy
routing_strategy.description: Effective strategy object (``{name, content_dependent, [timeout_ms], [parameters]}``), or ``None`` if the default ``weighted_random`` applies. ``content_dependent=True`` means final selection depends on the request body and the server can't pre-select — ``selected`` will be ``None``.

selected.description: Pre-selected candidate for content-independent strategies (weighted random, by-price, by-latency, ...). ``None`` when the strategy is content-dependent — the caller must supply a body to get a deterministic pick.

ResolveResponse.title: ResolveResponse
ResolveResponse.description: Dry-run result: candidate services, strategy, optional pre-pick.

SanitizedErrorInfo.title: SanitizedErrorInfo
SanitizedErrorInfo.description: Customer-safe error summary — no internal details (#882).

SecretOwnerTypeEnum.title: SecretOwnerTypeEnum
SecretOwnerTypeEnum.description: Owner type for secrets - determines which entity owns the secret.

name.title: Name
name.description: Secret name (e.g., OPENAI_API_KEY). Must be uppercase letters, digits, and underscores; must start with a letter or underscore.

SecretPublic.title: SecretPublic
SecretPublic.description: Public schema for secret info (excludes value - write-only).

value.title: Value
value.description: Secret value (will be encrypted). May be empty.

SecretUpdate.title: SecretUpdate
SecretUpdate.description:
Request body for ``PUT /secrets/{name}``.

Carries only the value — the name comes from the URL path. The same
schema is used for both create and update because ``PUT`` is
idempotent (see issue #798).

Empty string is allowed: a customer may deliberately store ``""``
to override a non-empty default in a ``${ secrets.X ?? default }``
reference. ``??`` coalesces on null only, so the explicit empty
value is preserved.

name.title: Name
name.description: URL-safe alias name (lowercase, alphanumeric, hyphens, underscores)

is_routing.title: Is Routing
is_routing.description: Whether to make this alias the active routing one for its (name, routing_key) combo. Fails with 409 if another alias is already routing.

ServiceAliasUpdate.title: ServiceAliasUpdate
ServiceAliasUpdate.description:
Schema for updating a ServiceAlias.

The alias name cannot be changed (it's the URL path segment).
All other fields — including request_routing_key — are updatable.
A unique constraint check runs at save time; conflicts return 409.

Routing state is managed via the dedicated ``POST /switch`` endpoint,
not through this schema.

ServiceCollectionMemberCreate.title: ServiceCollectionMemberCreate
ServiceCollectionMemberCreate.description: Schema for adding a member to a ServiceCollection.

ServiceCollectionMemberPublic.title: ServiceCollectionMemberPublic
ServiceCollectionMemberPublic.description: Public response model for a collection member.

ServiceDetail.title: ServiceDetail
ServiceDetail.description:
Full customer-facing service details.

Sourced from ``service_mview``. Intentionally narrow:

- Omits seller-internal fields (``seller_id``, ``routing_vars``,
  ``payout_price``, ``ops_subscription_id``, ``ops_customer_id``)
  and draft-only fields (``pending_revision_id``).
- Omits review stats (``review_count``, ``average_rating``) and
  logo URLs (``provider_logo``, ``offering_logo``,
  ``seller_logo``); those belong to the marketing/dashboard
  surface, not the SDK.
- Omits ``tagline`` as marketing copy not used for dispatch.

ServiceEnrollmentCreate.title: ServiceEnrollmentCreate
ServiceEnrollmentCreate.description:
Model for creating new Enrollments.

customer_id is derived from the X-Role-Id header at the route level.
service_id references the Service identity layer (not listing_id).

ServiceEnrollmentStatusEnum.title: ServiceEnrollmentStatusEnum
ServiceEnrollmentStatusEnum.description:
Status of a customer's service enrollment.

Workflow for services requiring parameters (user_parameters_schema exists):

    [not enrolled] → (enroll) → pending → (task completes) → incomplete → (configure) → active
                          │                                                                ↓
                          │                                  paused ← (pause) ←───────────┘
                          │
                          └─→ active  (if required secret already exists)

Workflow for services without parameters:

    [not enrolled] → (enroll) → pending → (task completes) → active
                                                                ↓
                                  paused ← (pause) ←──────────┘

States:
- pending: Celery task is processing (transient, show spinner)
- incomplete: Needs user configuration (required parameters not provided)
- active: Enrolled and fully configured
- paused: Enrolled but temporarily disabled
- cancelled: Enrollment cancelled

Secrets do not affect enrollment status — they are resolved at routing time.

ServiceSummary.title: ServiceSummary
ServiceSummary.description:
Minimal service info used when listing services inside a group.

Intentionally narrow: enough to render a picker and drill in,
nothing that customers don't need at browse time. Fetch
``ServiceDetail`` for the full schema.

``status`` is intentionally not exposed — customers only ever
see ``active`` services (the visibility filter guarantees it),
so a field whose only valid value is ``"active"`` carries no
information. Same reasoning for ``is_active`` on
``AccessInterface``.

demoted_alias_id.title: Demoted Alias Id
demoted_alias_id.description: ID of the sibling alias that was demoted when switching on. Null when switching off or when no sibling was routing.

UpstreamResponseInfo.title: UpstreamResponseInfo
UpstreamResponseInfo.description: Response received from the upstream service.
